Our Sacred Heritage

श्री स्वयंभू गणेश मंदिर

About the Temple

Nestled in the heart of Kandivali East, Mumbai, Shri Swayambhu Ganesh Temple has been a beacon of divine energy since its establishment in 1988. The presiding deity is a Swayambhu — a self-manifested idol, not crafted by human hands but naturally emerged from the earth itself, making it extraordinarily sacred.

The Shri Swayambhu Ganesh Trust (E.R. 21196) manages the temple with the utmost devotion and transparency. Over the decades, the temple has served millions of devotees, providing spiritual solace, conducting regular pujas, and supporting community welfare activities.

Your generous donation helps maintain the temple premises, support special festival celebrations, and preserve this divine heritage for future generations. Every rupee donated is accounted for, and you receive an official digital receipt for 80G tax deduction.

Choose Your Contribution

सहा दान प्रकार

Six Types of Donations

🐘
माघी गणेश जयंती
Maghi Ganesh Jayanti Donation
On the occasion of Maghi Ganesh Jayanti, donations are offered for special poojas, abhishek, and prasad distribution.
🪔
वार्षिक अभिषेक
Annual Abhisheka Donation
This donation supports the performance of special abhishekam rituals and religious ceremonies conducted throughout the year.
🔱
महाशिवरात्री
Maha Shivaratri Donation
During Maha Shivaratri, donations are used for special prayers, night-long worship, and devotional programs.
🌟
हनुमान जयंती
Hanuman Jayanti Donation
On Hanuman Jayanti, contributions help organize special poojas, bhajans, and prasad distribution for devotees.
🌸
आषाढी एकादशी
Ashadhi Ekadashi Donation
On the sacred occasion of Ashadhi Ekadashi, donations support special prayers, bhajans, and devotional activities dedicated to Lord Vitthal.
🏛
इतर दान
Other Donations
General donations are accepted for temple maintenance, development activities, and various religious and charitable initiatives.

Income Tax Benefit

80G

Tax Exemption — Income Tax Deduction Available

All donations to Shri Swayambhu Ganesh Trust (E.R. 21196) are eligible for tax deduction under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act. A digital receipt will be sent to your WhatsApp/Email after each donation.

Provide your address and PAN details for an official 80G certificate by post.

  • 01 Choose your Donation type, enter your custom amount, and complete the form.
  • 02 Make a secure payment via Razorpay — UPI, Netbanking, Cards, Wallets all accepted.
  • 03 Receive an instant digital receipt on your email and WhatsApp.
  • 04 Use the receipt to claim your 80G deduction while filing your Income Tax Return.
  • 05 Optionally request an official 80G certificate by providing your address and PAN number.
